terms.definition: The process by which internal human intentions and values are encoded into external artifacts or systems, thereby allowing those intentions to operate autonomously in the world.
The systemic degree to which the parts of a social, informational, or psychological system hold together in mutually reinforcing alignment.
A measure of disorder: in thermodynamics, unavailable energy; by analogy, the tendency of social or informational systems to drift toward disorder when unregulated.
A design strategy in which products are manufactured with deliberately short functional lifespans to encourage repeated consumer purchases.
The internal state of disorganization and unpredictability within an individual’s cognitive and emotional processes.
The reservoir of normative trust, shared values, and collective ethical commitments that enable a society to function cohesively.
The strength and stability of interpersonal and civic relationships within a population.
The reliability and trustworthiness of the information environment, including media and institutional communications.
